---
title: "DM_P004: Dual Parameter Sources Principle"
subtitle: "Use YAML for hierarchical config and Excel for tabular parameters"
chapter: "CH02"
category: "principle"
number: "DM_P004"
date-created: "2025-04-02"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "Data Management Law"
article: "Article 4"
derives_from:
  - "CH00.MP017: Separation of Concerns"
  - "CH00.MP011: Sensible Defaults"
  - "CH00.MP006: Data Source Hierarchy"
related_to:
  - "SO_P010: Config-Driven Customization"
  - "SO_R005: App YAML Configuration"
  - "DM_P001: Data Integrity"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**Data Management Law Article 4 (Dual Parameter Sources Principle)**

This article establishes that application parameters must be sourced from the most appropriate format based on their structure and purpose: YAML Configuration for hierarchical, simple, or unstructured parameters, and Excel (.xlsx) Files for tabular, relationship-driven, or structured parameters. All parameters, regardless of source, must be integrated into a unified configuration object during initialization.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Different parameter types have optimal storage formats. Use YAML for hierarchical configuration and developer-managed settings. Use Excel for tabular data and business-user-editable parameters. Integrate both into a unified configuration object at initialization.
:::

# Section 1: Rationale {#rationale}

## Subsection 1: Format Appropriateness {#format-appropriate}

This principle acknowledges that different parameter types have different natural representations:

1. **Format Appropriateness**: Each parameter type has an optimal storage format
2. **User Accessibility**: Excel files are more accessible to non-developers for certain parameters
3. **Data Structure Alignment**: Tabular data belongs in tabular files; hierarchical data in hierarchical files
4. **Integration Efficiency**: Loading all parameters at initialization creates a single source of truth
5. **Flexible Extensibility**: New parameters can be added in either format without code restructuring

## Subsection 2: Benefits {#benefits}

第一項：維護效率 (Maintenance efficiency)

- Business users can update Excel files without developer intervention
- Developers can modify YAML without affecting business data
- Clear separation of technical and business configuration
- Reduced errors from format mismatches

第二項：資料完整性 (Data integrity)

- Each format enforces appropriate structure
- Excel provides built-in validation for tabular data
- YAML supports complex hierarchical relationships
- Unified loading ensures consistency

# Section 2: Parameter Type Guidelines {#guidelines}

## Subsection 1: YAML-Appropriate Parameters {#yaml-params}

Store these parameters in app_config.yaml:

第一項：應用程式設定 (Application settings)

```yaml
# Application configuration
app:
  port: 3838
  mode: "production"
  debug: false
  timeout: 300
  max_upload_size: 100MB
```

第二項：功能旗標與階層設定 (Feature flags and hierarchical settings)

```yaml
# Feature toggles
features:
  advanced_analytics: true
  ai_recommendations: false
  export_functionality: true
  
# Nested configuration
ui:
  theme:
    primary_color: "#007bff"
    secondary_color: "#6c757d"
  layout:
    sidebar_width: 250
    header_height: 60
```

第三項：環境特定設定 (Environment-specific settings)

```yaml
# Environment configuration
environments:
  development:
    database: "dev_db"
    api_endpoint: "https://dev.api.example.com"
  production:
    database: "prod_db"
    api_endpoint: "https://api.example.com"
```

## Subsection 2: Excel-Appropriate Parameters {#excel-params}

Store these parameters in .xlsx files:

第一項：行銷通路與平台 (Marketing channels and platforms)

```
# platform.xlsx
| marketing_channel | marketing_channel_id | platform_code | is_active |
|------------------|---------------------|---------------|-----------|
| Amazon           | amazon              | AMZ           | TRUE      |
| Official Website | website             | WEB           | TRUE      |
| Retail Store     | retail              | RTL           | TRUE      |
| eBay            | ebay                | EBY           | FALSE     |
```

第二項：產品目錄 (Product catalogs)

```
# product_line.xlsx
| product_id | product_name | category | price | min_order | max_order |
|------------|-------------|----------|-------|-----------|-----------|
| PRD001     | Widget A    | Hardware | 19.99 | 1         | 100       |
| PRD002     | Widget B    | Hardware | 29.99 | 1         | 50        |
| PRD003     | Service X   | Service  | 99.99 | 1         | 10        |
```

第三項：UI 術語字典 (UI terminology dictionary)

```
# ui_terminology_dictionary.xlsx
| term_id | english | chinese | japanese | description |
|---------|---------|---------|----------|-------------|
| btn_submit | Submit | 提交 | 送信 | Submit button label |
| lbl_customer | Customer | 客戶 | 顧客 | Customer label |
| msg_success | Success | 成功 | 成功 | Success message |
```

# Section 3: Decision Framework {#decision-framework}

## Subsection 1: Structure Determinism {#structure}

第一項：結構決定因素 (Structure determination factors)

```r
# When structure is determined by code → YAML
sidebar_config:
  sections:
    - id: filters
      type: collapsible
      components: [date_range, channel_select]
    
# When structure is fixed but values change → Excel
# marketing_channels.xlsx with columns: name, id, active
```

第二項：編輯頻率與對象 (Edit frequency and audience)

Use Excel for configurations that:
- Require frequent updates by non-developers
- Benefit from spreadsheet-like editing
- Need to be handed off to business users

Use YAML for configurations that:
- Change rarely or only by developers
- Are closely tied to application architecture
- Require developer review for each change

## Subsection 2: Content Complexity {#complexity}

第一項：適合 Excel 的內容 (Content suitable for Excel)

```r
# Multi-row similar structure
products <- read_excel("products.xlsx")
# 100+ rows of product data

# Multi-language parallel content
translations <- read_excel("translations.xlsx")
# Columns: term_id, en, zh, ja, ko, etc.

# Complex relationships
mappings <- read_excel("category_mappings.xlsx")
# Old_category → New_category mappings
```

第二項：適合 YAML 的內容 (Content suitable for YAML)

```yaml
# Deeply nested structures
navigation:
  main:
    dashboard:
      overview:
        metrics: [sales, orders, customers]
      analytics:
        charts: [trend, distribution, comparison]
        
# Mixed property types
component:
  enabled: true
  refresh_rate: 30
  data_source: "primary"
  options:
    - value: 1
      label: "Option 1"
```

# Section 4: Implementation Pattern {#implementation}

## Subsection 1: Standard Loading Pattern {#loading}

第一項：統一載入函數 (Unified loading function)

```r
# Standard pattern for parameter loading
load_all_parameters <- function(yaml_path = "app_config.yaml") {
  # 1. Load YAML configuration
  config <- yaml::read_yaml(yaml_path)
  
  # 2. Determine parameters folder
  params_folder <- if (!is.null(config$brand$parameters_folder)) {
    config$brand$parameters_folder
  } else {
    file.path("app_data", "parameters")
  }
  
  # 3. Load Excel parameter files
  excel_files <- list.files(
    params_folder, 
    pattern = "\\.xlsx$", 
    full.names = TRUE
  )
  
  # 4. Initialize parameters section
  config$parameters <- list()
  
  # 5. Process each Excel file
  for (file_path in excel_files) {
    file_name <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file_path))
    
    tryCatch({
      # Load Excel file
      data <- readxl::read_excel(file_path)
      
      # Store in config
      config$parameters[[file_name]] <- data
      
      # Special processing for specific parameter types
      config <- process_special_parameters(config, file_name, data)
      
      message(sprintf("Loaded %d rows from %s", 
                      nrow(data), file_name))
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      warning(sprintf("Error loading parameter file %s: %s", 
                      file_name, e$message))
    })
  }
  
  return(config)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Special Parameter Processing {#processing}

第一項：特殊參數處理 (Special parameter processing)

```r
# Process special parameter types that need integration
process_special_parameters <- function(config, file_name, data) {
  
  # Platform-specific processing
  if (file_name == "platform") {
    if (all(c("marketing_channel", "marketing_channel_id") %in% 
            colnames(data))) {
      
      # Filter active channels
      active_data <- data[data$is_active == TRUE, ]
      
      # Create marketing channels list
      channels <- setNames(
        as.list(active_data$marketing_channel_id), 
        active_data$marketing_channel
      )
      
      config$company$marketing_channels <- channels
      
      if (length(channels) > 0) {
        config$company$default_channel <- names(channels)[1]
      }
    }
  }
  
  # UI terminology processing
  if (file_name == "ui_terminology_dictionary") {
    # Create fast lookup structure
    config$ui$terms <- list()
    
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(data))) {
      term_id <- data$term_id[i]
      config$ui$terms[[term_id]] <- as.list(data[i, ])
    }
  }
  
  # Product catalog processing
  if (file_name == "product_line") {
    # Create product lookup by ID
    config$products <- list()
    
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(data))) {
      product_id <- data$product_id[i]
      config$products[[product_id]] <- as.list(data[i, ])
    }
  }
  
  return(config)
}
```

# Section 5: Parameter Access Pattern {#access}

## Subsection 1: Consistent Access {#consistent-access}

第一項：存取模式 (Access patterns)

```r
# Access YAML-based configuration
debug_mode <- config$app$debug
app_language <- config$brand$language
feature_enabled <- config$features$advanced_analytics

# Access Excel-based parameters (pre-processed)
marketing_channels <- config$company$marketing_channels
default_channel <- config$company$default_channel

# Access Excel-based parameters (raw data frames)
product_df <- config$parameters$product_line
platform_df <- config$parameters$platform

# Access processed lookups
product_info <- config$products[["PRD001"]]
term_translation <- config$ui$terms[["btn_submit"]]$chinese
```

第二項：安全存取 (Safe access)

```r
# Safe parameter access with defaults
get_param <- function(config, path, default = NULL) {
  parts <- strsplit(path, "\\.")[[1]]
  value <- config
  
  for (part in parts) {
    if (is.null(value) || !part %in% names(value)) {
      return(default)
    }
    value <- value[[part]]
  }
  
  return(value)
}

# Usage
channels <- get_param(config, "company.marketing_channels", 
                      list("Default" = "default"))
debug <- get_param(config, "app.debug", FALSE)
```

# Section 6: Override Hierarchy {#override}

## Subsection 1: Precedence Order {#precedence}

Parameters can be overridden in the following order of precedence:

第一項：覆蓋優先順序 (Override priority order)

```r
# Load parameters with override support
load_config_with_overrides <- function() {
  # 1. Base YAML configuration (lowest priority)
  config <- yaml::read_yaml("app_config.yaml")
  
  # 2. Excel files override YAML
  config <- load_excel_parameters(config)
  
  # 3. Environment variables override Excel
  if (Sys.getenv("APP_DEBUG") != "") {
    config$app$debug <- as.logical(Sys.getenv("APP_DEBUG"))
  }
  
  if (Sys.getenv("APP_PORT") != "") {
    config$app$port <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("APP_PORT"))
  }
  
  # 4. Command-line arguments override everything (highest priority)
  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)
  if ("--debug" %in% args) {
    config$app$debug <- TRUE
  }
  
  if ("--port" %in% args) {
    port_idx <- which(args == "--port") + 1
    if (port_idx <= length(args)) {
      config$app$port <- as.integer(args[port_idx])
    }
  }
  
  return(config)
}
```

# Section 7: Best Practices {#best-practices}

## Subsection 1: File Organization {#organization}

```
app_data/
└── parameters/
    ├── platform.xlsx           # Marketing channels
    ├── product_line.xlsx       # Product catalog
    ├── ui_terminology.xlsx     # UI translations
    ├── calculation_rules.xlsx  # Business rules
    └── mappings.xlsx          # System mappings

app_config.yaml                # Main configuration
```

## Subsection 2: Documentation {#documentation}

第一項：參數文檔 (Parameter documentation)

```yaml
# Document YAML parameters
app:
  port: 3838  # Shiny app port (default: 3838)
  debug: false  # Enable debug mode (shows detailed errors)
  
# Document Excel structure in README
# platform.xlsx columns:
# - marketing_channel: Display name
# - marketing_channel_id: Internal ID
# - platform_code: 3-letter code
# - is_active: TRUE/FALSE for availability
```

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## Dual Parameter Sources Checklist

**Parameter Classification:**
- [ ] YAML for hierarchical configuration
- [ ] YAML for developer-managed settings
- [ ] Excel for tabular business data
- [ ] Excel for user-editable parameters

**Implementation:**
- [ ] Unified loading function implemented
- [ ] Special parameter processing added
- [ ] Safe access patterns used
- [ ] Override hierarchy respected

**File Organization:**
- [ ] Parameters folder structure created
- [ ] Excel files properly named
- [ ] YAML configuration documented
- [ ] Excel column structure documented

**Integration:**
- [ ] All parameters loaded at initialization
- [ ] Unified configuration object created
- [ ] Consistent access patterns
- [ ] Error handling for missing files

**Best Practices:**
- [ ] Clear separation of concerns
- [ ] Appropriate format for each parameter type
- [ ] Documentation for all parameters
- [ ] Version control considerations
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Dual Parameter Sources Principle optimizes parameter management by using the most appropriate format for each type of configuration. YAML handles hierarchical, developer-managed settings while Excel manages tabular, business-user-editable data. This separation improves maintainability, accessibility, and data integrity while providing a unified configuration interface for the application.

---

*Based on: Data Management Law Article 4*  
*Derives from: [MP017 Separation of Concerns](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP017_separation_of_concerns.qmd), [MP011 Sensible Defaults](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP011_sensible_defaults.qmd)*  
*Related to: [SO_P010 Config-Driven Customization](../../CH01_structure_organization/principles/SO_P010_config_driven_customization.qmd), [SO_R005 App YAML Configuration](../../CH01_structure_organization/rules/SO_R005_app_yaml_configuration.qmd)*
